error_severity_examples.detail: Cloze notetype card whose Text field contains no {{cN::}} deletion, so both question_text and answer_text render Anki's 'No cloze found on card' error instead of a studyable prompt; the card is broken.
Cloze note has no {{c1::}} deletion in its Text field, so the card renders Anki's error message instead of a studyable Q/A.

per_batch.notes: Batch 01 is a clean, uniform set of 258 MileDown cloze cards (General Chemistry, Biology, Biochemistry); the tag breadcrumb prefixing every question/answer is the expected template element and was not treated as an error. No card-breaking errors were found: every {{cN::...}} cloze is well-formed and balanced, HTML tags are reasonably balanced, there are no unrendered template artifacts, and no LaTeX/MathJax is used (math is shown via images and <sub>/<sup>). Findings are minor: 12 image-occlusion clozes whose deletion is an image so the answer mirrors the question with no textual reveal (info), plus 5 warnings in supplementary content that does not block studying (one cloze wrapping a stray <div>, three Extra reference links with an 'ahttps://' typo, and one empty href); three further image-cloze cards (photon-energy equation, periodic-table orbital labels, egg pathway) were not flagged because their Extra fields reveal real explanatory text.

Batch 02 is a large, uniform set of 258 MileDown cloze cards dominated by the 'Essential Equations' deck (Physics, Gen Chem) plus Biochem/Physics concept cards; the tag breadcrumb prefixing every question/answer is the expected template element and was not treated as an error. The only true error is one card (note 1554178673134) whose Text field has no {{cN::}} deletion, so it renders Anki's 'No cloze found' message; three Thermodynamics cards leak an empty '{{c2::}}' cloze from their Extra field into the answer (warning), and seven Extra-field YouTube links have malformed hrefs (stray "/doubled quotes) that still render but point to broken URLs (info). The bulk of findings (105) are image-only cloze cards where the equation/value/unit is an image, so answer_text mirrors question_text with no textual reveal beyond a link — these are functional image-occlusion-style cards flagged as info, consistent with batches 01/03/05; no LaTeX/MathJax delimiters are used (math is rendered via images and sub/sup).

Batch 03 is 257 MileDown cloze cards (Biochemistry/DNA-RNA, Behavioral neuroscience, and Physics fluids/electrostatics/magnetism/circuits) and is very clean: cloze {{cN::}} braces are balanced and well-formed, Q/A correspondence is accurate, and no LaTeX delimiters are used (equations are rendered as images). No errors were found. The few flags are low severity: three 'Give the equation' cards whose cloze deletion is an image so the plain-text answer reveals nothing (info), one cloze that awkwardly spans <div> tags (warning), and one Khan Academy link with a malformed href (info).

This batch of 257 MileDown MCAT cloze cards (Physics, Biology, Behavioral, Organic Chemistry) is high quality: cloze {{cN::...}} markup is balanced, HTML is reasonably balanced, no LaTeX/MathJax is present, and question/answer text is rendered cleanly with no unrendered template artifacts. The only genuine defect is one card whose Khan Academy link href has a stray " entity (broken source URL). Four 'give the equation' cards encode the answer solely as an image, so their answer_text contains no textual answer (info); a larger set of ~29 organic-chemistry 'identify the structure' cards are image-dependent on the question side but have valid text answers and balanced markup, so they were not flagged.

Batch 05 is 257 MileDown MCAT cloze cards (OChem isomers/bonding/reactions/alcohols/amino acids, Biology respiratory & cardiovascular systems, and Physics waves/sound/light/nuclear). It is very high quality: cloze {{cN::...}} markup, HTML, and Q/A correspondence are well-formed throughout, with zero errors or warnings and no unrendered template artifacts (equations are stored as images, not MathJax/LaTeX). The only flags are 13 info-level 'image-only' cloze cards whose deletion is a diagram or equation image; on 5 of these the Extra field adds no readable text, so the rendered answer text is essentially identical to the question prompt.

Batch 06 is 257 MileDown cloze cards (Physics nuclear phenomena, Behavioral psychology, Biology immune/digestive systems, and OChem carbonyl chemistry) and is overall very clean and well-formed. Only one true error exists: a note whose Text field lacks any cloze deletion, so it renders Anki's 'No cloze found' message instead of a studyable card. The remaining six flags are low-severity info notes: five 'Identify the...' OChem cards whose cloze deletion is an image (the plain-text answer reveals nothing) and one stray " inside a Khan Academy link URL.

This MileDown Behavioral/Biochemistry batch is very high quality: all 257 cloze cards have non-empty, readable question/answer text, balanced {{c1::...}} clozes, and deletions that correctly fill their blanks; no LaTeX/MathJax is used and no unrendered template artifacts were found. Only two minor issues exist: one Extra-field link with a malformed href (stray " entity) and one image-only cloze (Metabolic Pathways Overview) whose answer is a diagram with no text. A handful of other Extra-field links contain harmless double-encoded ampersands (&amp), which do not affect studying.

Batch 8 (257 MileDown cloze cards, all notetype Cloze-MileDown-b279e) is exceptionally clean: every card has balanced {{cN::}} cloze markup, reasonably balanced HTML, and no LaTeX/MathJax delimiters (the deck encodes formulas with HTML sub/sup and Unicode), with no unrendered template artifacts, empty answers, or Q/A correspondence failures. The only findings are four low-severity (info) text-quality nits: one spelling typo ('uninucelated'), one word split mid-string by an inline <b> tag, and two stems missing a small connective word. No error- or warning-level issues were found, and all cards are fully studyable with AI off.

Batch 9 is high-quality MileDown MCAT cloze content; 239/257 cards are clean with balanced cloze/LaTeX/HTML markup and correct question/answer correspondence, and no markup-balance or empty-answer errors were found. The dominant issue (16 cards, all in the Physics/Math scientific-notation, REDOX-balancing, and electrochemistry-equation sections) is image-only cloze answers where the deleted answer is rendered as an image and therefore absent from the extracted answer_text; these study correctly in-app and are recorded at info severity. The only non-image defects are one truncated sentence ('...after eating a meal while', warning) and one spacing artifact ('a rachidonic', info).

Batch 10 is a uniformly high-quality, all-cloze set from MileDown's MCAT decks (Physics/Research statistics, OChem spectroscopy & separations, Biochem lab techniques, and a large Behavioral block). Cloze {{c1::...}} markup, inline LaTeX \(..\), and HTML tags are balanced and well-formed throughout, question/answer correspondence is correct, and no unrendered template artifacts or image-only-without-text cards were found. Only two minor warnings: one card whose c1 cloze awkwardly wraps a
boundary (renders fine but malformed), and one whose stem says '9 principles' while listing only 8.

Batch 11 is a uniformly high-quality, all-cloze set drawn almost entirely from MileDown's Behavioral and Biology MCAT decks (psychology, neuroscience, development, and a few biology/physics items). Cloze {{c1::...}} markup and HTML tags net-balance throughout, all notes are single-cloze (c1) with correct stem/answer correspondence, there is no LaTeX/MathJax and no unrendered template artifacts. The only issues are minor: one cloze whose answer is an image-only deletion (FLAT PEG mnemonic), one broken reference-link href and one stray empty anchor, two missing-space merged words, and one card that ends on a dangling 'whereas'. A few harmless spelling/anchor typos (e.g. 'enviromental', 'intensions', 'YouTub Link') were noted but not individually flagged as they remain readable.

Batch 12 is a uniformly high-quality, all-cloze set drawn from MileDown's Behavioral deck (~54 cards, with tag breadcrumbs + image/link Extras) and a large Pankow P/S block (8C Social Behavior/Interactions, Biological Explanations, 9A Social Structures, 9B Demographics). Q/A correspondence is correct throughout, text is well-formed and non-empty with no unrendered template artifacts ({{, FrontSide, etc.), no LaTeX is present, and no image-only-without-text cards were found. The only issues are two cards where a closing </font> tag is authored inside the cloze hint (after the :: separator), leaving an orphaned/unclosed font tag on the rendered side; both are minor warnings that render acceptably.

This batch is a single hand-authored Pankow P/S (psych/sociology) cloze deck and is of very high quality: all 257 cards are Cloze-8d4a6 with balanced {{cN::...}} markup, balanced HTML (b/u/div/font) tags, no LaTeX/MathJax, and clean Q/A correspondence where the revealed deletion fits the blank. No errors or warnings were found. The only flag is one info-level card (1582507853930) whose c1 deletion is an image with no text, making the tested content image-only even though the Extra field still provides readable answer text.

Batch 14 is a single hand-authored Pankow P/S (psych/sociology + sensation/perception) cloze deck, split between Cloze-8d4a6 and AACloze notetypes, and is of very high structural quality. All 257 cards passed the three checks: Q/A correspondence is clean (every cloze deletion reveals text that fits the blank or its [hint]; no empty answers and no answer==question), text is well-formed and readable with no unrendered template artifacts ('{{', '{{c1::', 'FrontSide'), and the raw fields have balanced {{cN::...}} markup plus balanced HTML (b/u/i/sub/sup/div/font) tags, with no LaTeX/MathJax present. Notably, every cloze deletion contains real text and all images live in the Extra field, so there are no image-only deletions or image-only cards to flag. The only imperfections are out-of-scope content issues (spelling typos like 'percieve'/'Austism'/'Hypocritin', informal/crude mnemonics, and one card whose author-noted mesolimbic/mesocortical mapping is self-questioned), none of which break studying.

Batch 15 is drawn entirely from the MrPankow 6A/6B P/S cloze deck (all AACloze notes) and is in excellent structural shape: cloze {{cN::...}} markup, embedded <font>/<b>/<u>/<i> HTML, and HTML entities ( , >) are balanced and well-formed across all 257 cards, with correct Q/A correspondence and no unrendered template artifacts ('{{', 'FrontSide', etc.). No LaTeX/MathJax is present in this batch, so delimiter checks are not applicable. The only flagged item is one card whose front includes a stray 'example?' prompt answered only via the Extra field; numerous content-level spelling typos exist throughout (e.g., 'seperating', 'recieves', 'percieve', 'declerative', 'Repalse', 'oribofrontal') but they leave the text readable/studyable and are out of scope for these structural checks.

Batch 16 is drawn entirely from the MrPankow 6B/6C P/S cloze deck (all 257 cards are AACloze notes) and is in excellent structural shape: every {{cN::...}} deletion is balanced and well-formed, each revealed answer correctly corresponds to its blanked front, and there are no empty answers, no unrendered template artifacts ('{{', '}}', 'FrontSide'), and no LaTeX/MathJax (so delimiter checks are not applicable). The embedded HTML (deeply nested
//// plus  /> entities and tags) is messy but consistently balanced, and image-bearing cards always retain usable cloze text. The only flagged items are two cosmetic split-word artifacts ('compreh end') on note 1590817290222; numerous content-level spelling typos exist throughout (e.g., 'duel coding', 'declerative', 'Conjuction', 'Accomodation') but they remain readable/studyable and are out of scope for these structural checks.

Batch 17 is a clean, uniform set of 257 single/dual cloze cards (AACloze) from the MrPankow P/S Deck (units 6B, 6C, 7A). Cloze {{cN::...}} braces are well-formed and balanced (including the ::hint syntax that renders as [Brain region], [Theory], etc.), HTML tags (font/div/i/b/u/img/br) are balanced even inside the multi-line cloze 'theory of emotion' cards, and no LaTeX/MathJax delimiters are used (figures live in the Extra field as images). Q/A correspondence is correct throughout with no empty answers, no answer==question, no unrendered template artifacts, and no image-only/image-occlusion cards. The only flagged card is one tautological 'midbrain becomes the midbrain' note whose back is throwaway profanity; a handful of unrelated spelling typos (e.g., 'cerebullum', 'temportal', 'Percieved', 'disappeasra') and crude/informal Extra notes exist but do not break studying, so they were not flagged.

Batch 18 is a uniformly high-quality set of 257 single/dual cloze cards (all AACloze from one source deck, MrPankow P/S Deck 7A); cloze {{c1}}/{{c2}} braces are well-formed and balanced (including the ::hint syntax that renders as [Theory], [Dude], [hormone], etc.), HTML tags (font/b/i/u/div/img/br) are balanced even in the multi-deletion id/ego/superego and defense-mechanism notes, and no LaTeX/MathJax delimiters are used (figures live as images in the Extra field). Q/A correspondence is correct throughout both ords with no empty answers, no answer==question, no unrendered template artifacts, and no image-only cards. The only flags are 4 info-level cosmetic artifacts where mnemonics bold individual leading letters (OCEAN and 'AM I Motivated'), splitting words in the rendered answer; unrelated source spelling typos (e.g., 'reservour', 'plateu', 'immitation', 'fullfilment') and crude/informal notes exist but fall outside the three structural checks and do not break studying, so they were not flagged.

Batch 19 is a uniform, single-source set of 257 AACloze cards from the MrPankow P/S (psychology/sociology) deck and is uniformly clean with no errors, warnings, or info findings. Every cloze {{cN::...}} is balanced and well-formed (including valid hint syntax like {{c1::Panic disorders::Illness}} and {{c1::large::large or small}}), all HTML in the Text/Extra fields is reasonably balanced, and there is no LaTeX/MathJax to validate; all cloze deletions are text (every lives in the Extra field, so there are no image-only cloze cards), so each answer_text correctly reveals its [...] blank and no card has empty/duplicate or non-corresponding answers. The only non-blocking observations (intentionally not flagged) are occasional source-level spelling typos (e.g., 'behavoirs', 'shizoid', 'Cheynes-stroke', 'seperate') and informal/profane phrasing, none of which affect studyability.

Batch 20 is a uniform, single-source set of 257 AACloze cards from the MrPankow P/S (psychology/sociology) deck, spanning the 7C and 8A/8B subdecks, and is uniformly clean with no errors, warnings, or info findings. Every cloze {{cN::...}} is balanced and well-formed (including valid hint syntax such as {{c1::B.F. Skinner::dude}}, {{c1::...::in terms of stimuli}}, and {{c1::Pre-conventional::Stage}}), all inline HTML in the Text/Extra fields (b/i/u/font/div nesting) is reasonably balanced, and there is no LaTeX/MathJax to validate. All cloze deletions are text and every lives in the Extra field, so there are no image-only cloze cards; each answer_text correctly reveals its [...] (or hinted) blank with no empty, duplicate, or non-corresponding answers. The only non-blocking observations (intentionally not flagged) are occasional source-level typos (e.g., 'grouple' on card 1592264560445, 'stimuli'/'recieves'/'seperate'/'percieves'), informal or profane example wording, and several Erikson-stage cards whose trailing 'mnemonic:' label is fulfilled by an Extra image rather than text — none of which break studyability.
